description: High-impact ITN CEA parameters identified from Phase 2 red-team findings and spreadsheet mapping

parameters.description: Reduction in malaria incidence for children under 5 receiving nets (Pryce et al. meta-analysis). Findings 1,2,7: may overstate due to mosquito species composition changes, housing quality differences, and behavioral adaptation.

Adjustment for net usage in program vs. trial contexts. Applied multiplicatively to trial usage rate (0.7). Finding 3: post-distribution usage decline may be steeper than modeled; average usage over lifespan could be 55-60% vs 70%.

Adjustment for insecticide resistance reducing net effectiveness. Quantifier Finding: temporal degradation of pyrethroid susceptibility since trial period means current adjustments may understate resistance by 20-40%.
Indirect malaria deaths per direct malaria death. Finding 6: if verbal autopsy misclassification inflates baseline malaria mortality by 20-40%, this ratio may need downward revision.

External validity adjustment capturing systematic differences between trial and implementation settings. Finding 2: a generic 5% adjustment may be insufficient for housing quality effects (16-29% CE reduction) and other context differences.
Relative efficacy of ITNs for reducing mortality in people age 5+. Synthesizer ungrounded hypothesis: no RCTs estimate bed net impact on over-5 mortality; the 0.8 assumption lacks direct evidence.
